Button driver interface for initialization, state checking, interrupt handling, and self-testing.

# Purpose
This C header file declares functions and includes necessary headers for managing button inputs in an embedded system. The [`button_init`](<#button_init>) function initializes the button hardware. The [`button_is_pressed`](<#button_is_pressed>) function checks if a button, identified by `ButtonId`, is currently pressed. The [`button_get_state_bits`](<#button_get_state_bits>) function returns the state of all buttons as a bitmask. The [`button_interrupt_handler`](<#button_interrupt_handler>) function handles interrupts triggered by button events, taking a pointer to a `button_id` as an argument. The [`button_selftest`](<#button_selftest>) function performs a self-test to verify the functionality of the button system. The file includes the `button_id.h` header and standard libraries for boolean and integer types.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `button_id.h`
- `stdbool.h`
- `stdint.h`
